metadata.title: Transparency and Targeting of Political Advertisement Vocabulary

description.en: The website of the Public Event.

description.en: Information describing the financial aspects of a political campaign,including sources of funding,expenditures,and financial disclosures.

usage.en: Use this class to provide aggregated financial information for a political advertising campaign, including total amount and currency, as required for transparency and reporting under EU Regulation 2024/900.

rawTags.definition-en: Information describing the financial aspects of a political campaign,including sources of funding,expenditures,and financial disclosures.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to provide aggregated financial information for a political advertising campaign, including total amount and currency, as required for transparency and reporting under EU Regulation 2024/900.

description.en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.

usage.en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency.

rawTags.definition-en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.
rawTags.usageNote-en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency.

description.en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.

usage.en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

rawTags.definition-en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.
rawTags.usageNote-en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

description.en: An event in which eligible participants formally express a choice or decision to elect representatives or decide on public matters, typically through voting.

rawTags.definition-en: An event in which eligible participants formally express a choice or decision to elect representatives or decide on public matters, typically through voting.

description.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

rawTags.definition-en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

description.en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Indicate the country where the vote is taking place, using the official name or code.

rawTags.definition-en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Indicate the country where the vote is taking place, using the official name or code.

description.en: A name given to the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the vote.

rawTags.definition-en: A name given to the resource.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the vote.

description.en: An unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.

usage.en:
Provide a unique identifier for the vote within the relevant context.
Recommended practice is to identify the resource by means of a string conforming to an identification system. Examples include International Standard Book Number (ISBN), Digital Object Identifier (DOI), and Uniform Resource Name (URN). Persistent identifiers should be provided as HTTP URIs.

rawTags.definition-en: An unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.
rawTags.usageNote-en:
Provide a unique identifier for the vote within the relevant context.
Recommended practice is to identify the resource by means of a string conforming to an identification system. Examples include International Standard Book Number (ISBN), Digital Object Identifier (DOI), and Uniform Resource Name (URN). Persistent identifiers should be provided as HTTP URIs.

description.en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.

usage.en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the vote is conducted.

rawTags.definition-en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the vote is conducted.

description.en: Financial support provided for a project, activity, or entity, typically by an organisation, government, or individual.

usage.en: Use this class to represent the overall financial structure supporting a political advertisement or campaign, including both direct and indirect funding sources, as required for transparency under EU Regulation 2024/900.

rawTags.definition-en: Financial support provided for a project, activity, or entity, typically by an organisation, government, or individual.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to represent the overall financial structure supporting a political advertisement or campaign, including both direct and indirect funding sources, as required for transparency under EU Regulation 2024/900.

description.en: Financial information related to the political advertisement.

rawTags.definition-en: Financial information related to the political advertisement.

description.en: Financial information related to the campaign.

rawTags.definition-en: Financial information related to the campaign.

description.en: The method or approach used to calculate the amount or allocation of funding.

usage.en: Describe the formula, standard, or process used to determine the funding amount.

rawTags.definition-en: The method or approach used to calculate the amount or allocation of funding.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Describe the formula, standard, or process used to determine the funding amount.

description.en: A project,program,or organised effort undertaken to achieve specific objectives, often in a political, legislative, or public context.

usage.en: Use this class to describe a legislative or regulatory initiative that is referenced or promoted by a political advertisement. This enables transparency about the link between the advertisement and ongoing or proposed political processes, as required by EU Regulation 2024/900.

rawTags.definition-en: A project,program,or organised effort undertaken to achieve specific objectives, often in a political, legislative, or public context.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to describe a legislative or regulatory initiative that is referenced or promoted by a political advertisement. This enables transparency about the link between the advertisement and ongoing or proposed political processes, as required by EU Regulation 2024/900.

description.en: An advertisement with a political subject matter,including information about its content,format,language,publication dates,and links to legislative processes or votes.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any advertisement with political content,including its metadata and links to related legislative or voting processes.

rawTags.definition-en: An advertisement with a political subject matter,including information about its content,format,language,publication dates,and links to legislative processes or votes.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to represent any advertisement with political content,including its metadata and links to related legislative or voting processes.

description.en: The end of a period or interval.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination ends.

rawTags.definition-en: The end of a period or interval.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination ends.

description.en: The beginning of a period or interval.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination starts.

rawTags.definition-en: The beginning of a period or interval.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination starts.

description.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en:
Use a controlled vocabulary for the format of the advertisement.
Recommended practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the DCMI Type Vocabulary [DCMI-TYPE]. To describe the file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource, use the property Format.

rawTags.definition-en: The nature or genre of the resource.
rawTags.usageNote-en:
Use a controlled vocabulary for the format of the advertisement.
Recommended practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the DCMI Type Vocabulary [DCMI-TYPE]. To describe the file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource, use the property Format.

description.en: A topic of the resource.

usage.en:
Provide the main topic or subject matter of the advertisement.
Recommended practice is to refer to the subject with a URI. If this is not possible or feasible, a literal value that identifies the subject may be provided. Both should preferably refer to a subject in a controlled vocabulary.

rawTags.usageNote-en:
Provide the main topic or subject matter of the advertisement.
Recommended practice is to refer to the subject with a URI. If this is not possible or feasible, a literal value that identifies the subject may be provided. Both should preferably refer to a subject in a controlled vocabulary.
rawTags.definition-en: A topic of the resource.

description.en: A language of the resource.

usage.en:
Indicate the language in which the political advertisement is presented to the public (e.g., the spoken or written language of the advertisement content).
Recommended practice is to use either a non-literal value representing a language from a controlled vocabulary such as ISO 639-2 or ISO 639-3, or a literal value consisting of an IETF Best Current Practice 47 [IETF-BCP47] language tag.

rawTags.definition-en: A language of the resource.
rawTags.usageNote-en:
Indicate the language in which the political advertisement is presented to the public (e.g., the spoken or written language of the advertisement content).
Recommended practice is to use either a non-literal value representing a language from a controlled vocabulary such as ISO 639-2 or ISO 639-3, or a literal value consisting of an IETF Best Current Practice 47 [IETF-BCP47] language tag.

description.en: TODO

usage.en: $ea_notes=A Legal Entity able to transact business, typically registered with a body able to confer legal status such as a national business register. It is able to trade, is legally liable for its actions, accounts, tax affairs etc. This makes Legal Entities distinct from the concept of organisations or groups. Many organisations exist that are not Legal Entities, yet to the outside world they have staff, hierarchies, locations etc. Other organisations exist that are an umbrella for several Legal Entities (universities are often good examples of this). This vocabulary is concerned solely with registered Legal Entities and does not attempt to cover all possible trading bodies.

rawTags.definition-en: null
rawTags.usageNote-en: $ea_notes=A Legal Entity able to transact business, typically registered with a body able to confer legal status such as a national business register. It is able to trade, is legally liable for its actions, accounts, tax affairs etc. This makes Legal Entities distinct from the concept of organisations or groups. Many organisations exist that are not Legal Entities, yet to the outside world they have staff, hierarchies, locations etc. Other organisations exist that are an umbrella for several Legal Entities (universities are often good examples of this). This vocabulary is concerned solely with registered Legal Entities and does not attempt to cover all possible trading bodies.

description.en: A record providing information about a political advertisement, its funding, sponsors, targeting, and related entities, to ensure transparency and compliance with legal requirements.

usage.en: Use this class as the root element for transparency disclosures related to political advertising.

description.en: A self-employed person, company, or organization that has legal rights and obligations.

usage.en: A Legal Entity able to transact business, typically registered with a body able to confer legal status such as a national business register. It is able to trade, is legally liable for its actions, accounts, tax affairs etc. This makes Legal Entities distinct from the concept of organisations or groups. Many organisations exist that are not Legal Entities, yet to the outside world they have staff, hierarchies, locations etc. Other organisations exist that are an umbrella for several Legal Entities (universities are often good examples of this). This vocabulary is concerned solely with registered Legal Entities and does not attempt to cover all possible trading bodies.

description.en: A self-employed person,company,or organization that has legal rights and obligations.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any legal entity involved in the context of a political advertisement, including the sponsor, the paying entity, or the legal entity ultimately controlling the sponsor. This class supports the requirements for transparency notices under EU Regulation 2024/900, which mandate disclosure of the identity, contact details, and registration information for all such entities.

rawTags.definition-en: A self-employed person,company,or organization that has legal rights and obligations.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use this class to represent any legal entity involved in the context of a political advertisement, including the sponsor, the paying entity, or the legal entity ultimately controlling the sponsor. This class supports the requirements for transparency notices under EU Regulation 2024/900, which mandate disclosure of the identity, contact details, and registration information for all such entities.

description.en: The name of a natural person, typically composed of given name(s) and family name(s).

usage.en: Used only when the entity is an individual; not applicable for organisations.

rawTags.usageNote-en: Used only when the entity is an individual; not applicable for organisations.
rawTags.definition-en: The name of a natural person, typically composed of given name(s) and family name(s).

description.en: The name under which the Legal Entity is legally registered.

usage.en:
Provide the name under which the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity is legally registered.
The legal name is different from the alternative (trade) name which is a pseudonym used by companies that do not operate under their registered company name.
A business might have more than one legal name, particularly in countries with more than one official language. In such cases, and where the encoding technology allows, the language of the string should be identified.

rawTags.definition-en: The name under which the Legal Entity is legally registered.
rawTags.usageNote-en:
Provide the name under which the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity is legally registered.
The legal name is different from the alternative (trade) name which is a pseudonym used by companies that do not operate under their registered company name.
A business might have more than one legal name, particularly in countries with more than one official language. In such cases, and where the encoding technology allows, the language of the string should be identified.

description.en: A string of characters to uniquely identify a concept.

usage.en: Provide a string of characters to uniquely identify the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity.

rawTags.usageNote-en: Provide a string of characters to uniquely identify the legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity.

description.en: A spatial object that in a human-readable way identifies a fixed location.

usage.en:
An "address representation" as conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Address Representation data type: "Representation of an address spatial object for use in external application schemas that need to include the basic, address information in a readable way.".

The representation of Addresses varies widely from one country's postal system to another. Even within countries, there are almost always examples of Addresses that do not conform to the stated national standard. However, ISO 19160-1 provides a method through which different Addresses can be converted from one conceptual model to another.

This specification was heavily based on the INSPIRE Address Representation data type. It is noteworthy that if an Address is provided using the detailed breakdown suggested by the properties for this class, then it will be INSPIRE-conformant. To this very granular set of properties, we add two further properties:

- full address (the complete address as a formatted string)
- addressID (a unique identifier for the address).

The first of these allows publishers to simply provide the complete Address as one string, with or without formatting. This is analogous to vCard's label property.

The addressID is part of the INSPIRE guidelines and provides a hook that can be used to link the Address to an alternative representation, such as vCard or OASIS xAL.

This class belongs to Core Location Vocabulary

rawTags.definition-en: A spatial object that in a human-readable way identifies a fixed location.

The representation of Addresses varies widely from one country's postal system to another. Even within countries, there are almost always examples of Addresses that do not conform to the stated national standard. However, ISO 19160-1 provides a method through which different Addresses can be converted from one conceptual model to another.

This specification was heavily based on the INSPIRE Address Representation data type. It is noteworthy that if an Address is provided using the detailed breakdown suggested by the properties for this class, then it will be INSPIRE-conformant. To this very granular set of properties, we add two further properties:

- full address (the complete address as a formatted string)
- addressID (a unique identifier for the address).

The first of these allows publishers to simply provide the complete Address as one string, with or without formatting. This is analogous to vCard's label property.

The addressID is part of the INSPIRE guidelines and provides a hook that can be used to link the Address to an alternative representation, such as vCard or OASIS xAL.

This class belongs to Core Location Vocabulary

description.en: The name of a passage or way through from one location to another.

usage.en: A thoroughfare is usually a street, but it might be a waterway or some other feature. For example, "Avenue des Champs-Élysées".

rawTags.definition-en: The name of a passage or way through from one location to another.
rawTags.usageNote-en: A thoroughfare is usually a street, but it might be a waterway or some other feature. For example, "Avenue des Champs-Élysées".

description.en: A name created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.

usage.en: Usually a city, for example "Paris".

rawTags.definition-en: A name created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Usually a city, for example "Paris".

description.en: The code created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.

usage.en: Post codes are common elements in many countries' postal address systems. One of the many post codes of Paris is for example "75000".

rawTags.definition-en: The code created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Post codes are common elements in many countries' postal address systems. One of the many post codes of Paris is for example "75000".

description.en: Proper noun(s) applied to the real world entity identified by the locator. The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

usage.en:
The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

The key difference between a locator designator and a locator name is that the latter is a proper name and is unlikely to include digits. For example, "Shumann, Berlaymont" is a meeting room within the European Commission headquarters for which locator name is more appropriate than locator.

rawTags.usageNote-en:
The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

The key difference between a locator designator and a locator name is that the latter is a proper name and is unlikely to include digits. For example, "Shumann, Berlaymont" is a meeting room within the European Commission headquarters for which locator name is more appropriate than locator.
rawTags.definition-en: Proper noun(s) applied to the real world entity identified by the locator. The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

description.en: The complete address written as a string.

usage.en: Use of this property is recommended as it will not suffer any misunderstandings that might arise through the breaking up of an address into its component parts. This property is analogous to vCard's label property but with two important differences: (1) formatting is not assumed so that, unlike vCard label, it may not be suitable to print this on an address label, (2) vCard's label property has a domain of vCard Address; the fullAddress property has no such restriction. An example of a full address is "Champ de Mars, 5 Avenue Anatole France, 75007 Paris, France".

rawTags.definition-en: The complete address written as a string.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Use of this property is recommended as it will not suffer any misunderstandings that might arise through the breaking up of an address into its component parts. This property is analogous to vCard's label property but with two important differences: (1) formatting is not assumed so that, unlike vCard label, it may not be suitable to print this on an address label, (2) vCard's label property has a domain of vCard Address; the fullAddress property has no such restriction. An example of a full address is "Champ de Mars, 5 Avenue Anatole France, 75007 Paris, France".

description.en: The name of a secondary level/region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.

usage.en: Values could be a region or province, more granular than level 1.

rawTags.definition-en: The name of a secondary level/region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Values could be a region or province, more granular than level 1.

description.en: The name of the uppermost level of the address, almost always a country.

usage.en: Best practice is to use the ISO 3166-1 code but if this is inappropriate for the context, country names should be provided in a consistent manner to reduce ambiguity. For example, either write 'France' or 'FRA' consistently throughout the dataset and avoid mixing the two. The Country controlled vocabulary from the Publications Office can be reused for this.

rawTags.definition-en: The name of the uppermost level of the address, almost always a country.
rawTags.usageNote-en: Best practice is to use the ISO 3166-1 code but if this is inappropriate for the context, country names should be provided in a consistent manner to reduce ambiguity. For example, either write 'France' or 'FRA' consistently throughout the dataset and avoid mixing the two. The Country controlled vocabulary from the Publications Office can be reused for this.

description.en: A globally unique identifier for each instance of an Address.

usage.en:
The concept of adding a globally unique identifier for each instance of an address is a crucial part of the INSPIRE data spec. A number of EU countries have already implemented an ID (a UUID) in their Address Register/gazetteer, among them Denmark. OASIS xAL also includes an address identifier. It is the address Identifier that allows an address to be represented in a format other than INSPIRE whilst remaining conformant to the Core Vocabulary.

The INSPIRE method of representing addresses is very detailed, designed primarily for use in databases of addresses. Whilst data that is published in full conformance with the INSPIRE data structure can be made available using theCore Location Vocabulary the reverse is not true since the Core Vocabulary allows much greater flexibility.

Many datasets that include address data as one piece of information about something else are likely to have that data in simpler formats. These might be tailored to the specific need of the dataset, follow a national norm, or make use of a standard like vCard.

To provide maximum flexibility in the Core Vocabulary, whilst remaining interoperable with INSPIRE Address Guidelines (which EU Member States are obliged to use), the Core Location Vocabulary provides the extra property of full address and makes use of INSPIRE's addressID.

It is the address Identifier that allows an address to be represented in a format other than INSPIRE whilst remaining conformant to the Core Vocabulary.

To provide maximum flexibility in the Core Vocabulary, whilst remaining interoperable with INSPIRE Address Guidelines (which EU Member States are obliged to use), the Core Location Vocabulary provides the extra property of full address and makes use of INSPIRE's addressID.

description.en: Entity that is able to carry out action.

usage.en: In compliance with the description from FOAF, an Agent is considered as any entity that is able to carry out actions. The Agent class acts as a generic element which can be further specified by implementers for their usages, for example by defining the Person class from the Core Person Vocabulary or Organization from W3C's Organization Ontology as a subclasses of Agent. This Person or Organization can then issue a certain Requirement or be concerned by an Evidence provided.

This Person or Organization can then issue a certain Requirement or be concerned by an Evidence provided.

usage.en: For individuals, only if isAddressPublic=true; for organisations, use as the main correspondence address.

description.en: The name under which the Legal Entity is legally registered.

usage.en:
Provide the name under which the controller, legal entity, sponsor, or paying entity is legally registered.
The legal name is different from the alternative (trade) name which is a pseudonym used by companies that do not operate under their registered company name.
A business might have more than one legal name, particularly in countries with more than one official language. In such cases, and where the encoding technology allows, the language of the string should be identified.

description.en: The text data type is a combination of a string and a language identifier.

usage.en:
It is useful for names and descriptions that are available in multiple languages. Where this is so, each version of the data should be included and each one associated with the relevant language identifier. RFC 3066 provides a commonly used set of identifiers for natural languages. This is the set recognised by UN/CEFACT and XML Schema.
Languages are represented by two character codes, optionally followed by a locale definition such as "de" meaning German and "de-at" meaning "German as spoken in Austria."

description.en: Date represents top-open intervals of exactly one day in length on the timelines of dateTime, beginning on the beginning moment of each day, up to but not including the beginning moment of the next day).  For non-timezoned values, the top-open intervals disjointly cover the non-timezoned timeline, one per day.  For timezoned values, the intervals begin at every minute and therefore overlap.

description.en: Integer is ·derived· from decimal by fixing the value of ·fractionDigits· to be 0 and disallowing the trailing decimal point.  This results in the standard mathematical concept of the integer numbers.  The ·value space· of integer is the infinite set {...,-2,-1,0,1,2,...}.  The ·base type· of integer is decimal.
